aliases: Starbucks VIA instant coffee vanilla latte

english: Starbucks VIA instant coffee vanilla latte

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for instant vanilla latte powder sachets; closest parent 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301242) used as the ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for flavoured instant latte sachets.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 'Coffee (substance)' is the closest available term; no specific concept exists for instant vanilla latte powder.

serving.description: 1 stick sachet (approximately 21 g) prepared with hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One stick sachet (approximately 21 g) dissolved in 237 mL of hot water constitutes a single serving of Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte — Nutrition Facts (USDA Branded Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte instant coffee powder, expressed per 100 g of powder. One stick sachet is approximately 21 g. Per-100 g values calculated from the per-serving label data (energy 80 kcal per 21 g serving; protein 2 g; fat 1.5 g; saturated fat 1 g; carbohydrate 15 g; sugar 13 g; dietary fibre 0 g; sodium 95 mg per serving).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte instant powder. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the analogous food 'instant coffee with sugar and milk' from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The dominant glycemic driver is sucrose/sugar content (~62 g per 100 g powder). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 15) / 100 = 8.25, rounded to 8.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content in powder form (452 mg per 100 g; approximately 95 mg per serving) and caffeine stimulation of blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one serving per day and monitor total daily sodium intake, as each sachet contributes approximately 95 mg sodium alongside caffeine, which may transiently elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar content (61.9 g sugar per 100 g powder; approximately 13 g per serving) with a low glycemic index of 55 and glycemic load of 8 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this product with caution: each sachet delivers approximately 13 g of added sugar, contributing meaningfully to postprandial glucose excursions, and regular consumption is inconsistent with tight glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (4.8 g per 100 g powder; approximately 1 g per serving) and high sugar load contributing to dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this product to occasional use, as the combination of saturated fat from dairy creamer and high added sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed habitually.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte is an ultra-processed instant coffee beverage sold in individual stick sachets, with a standard retail pack containing four sticks. Each sachet (approximately 21 g) is dissolved in hot water to produce a creamy, sweet vanilla-flavoured latte. The product is formulated from instant coffee, skim milk powder, sugar, natural vanilla flavour, and a dairy-based creamer, delivering a café-style latte experience without espresso equipment. The flavour profile is sweet and milky with a pronounced vanilla note and mild coffee bitterness. Nutritionally, the powder is energy-dense at approximately 381 kcal per 100 g, with a high sugar content of approximately 62 g per 100 g (13 g per serving), moderate sodium at 452 mg per 100 g (95 mg per serving), and modest protein at 9.5 g per 100 g. The product contains milk allergens and is not suitable for vegans or those avoiding dairy. From a clinical perspective, the high added sugar content makes this product a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome, and the sodium and caffeine content warrant attention for those with hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous instant coffee-with-milk-and-sugar data from the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 8 per serving. In Singapore, this product is sold at Starbucks outlets and via field survey at approximately SGD 8.70 for a 4-stick pack.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are calculated from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods per-serving label data for Starbucks VIA Vanilla Latte, scaled to per-100 g basis. This is an exact branded product match in an approved secondary global source (USDA Branded Foods). GI is an analogous estimate based on the closest approved GI source entry for instant coffee with milk and sugar; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Per-100 g nutrient values are derived by scaling from the per-serving (21 g sachet) label data reported in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods: energy 80 kcal, protein 2 g, fat 1.5 g, saturated fat 1 g, trans fat 0 g, carbohydrate 15 g, sugar 13 g, dietary fibre 0 g, sodium 95 mg per serving. Dietary fibre is 0.0 g per 100 g — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods label; this is consistent with the product formulation (no fibre-containing ingredients). Trans fat is 0.0 g per 100 g — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods label. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, and all vitamins and minerals beyond sodium are -1 as they are not reported in the USDA Branded Foods label for this product. Water content is -1 as this is a dry powder product and water content is not reported in the source. GI is an analogous estimate of 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for instant coffee with milk and sugar; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific Starbucks product. Glycemic load per serving = (55 × 15) / 100 = 8.25, rounded to 8.0. The product is sold in Singapore via field survey at Starbucks - One Fullerton at SGD 8.70 for a 4-stick pack. Halal certification status for Singapore is not confirmed from approved sources and is set to false pending verification. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is a beverage product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full RESULT JSON. Identified that dietary_fibre_g=0.0 and trans_fat_g=0.0 required zero justification phrases in notes per the zero-macro justification rule. Identified that all -1 sentinel values needed documentation.

Iteration 2: Added explicit confirmed-zero documentation for dietary_fibre_g and trans_fat_g in notes. Added documentation for all -1 sentinel values (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, all vitamins, water_g) per the sentinel values documentation rule. Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as glycemic_index 55 falls into the low GI range (55 or less). Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Confirmed all hard gates pass and score is 8/8.
